modules.title: Crimson Desert Release Date and Platforms
modules.summary: Crimson Desert is available now. It launched on March 19, 2026 as a single-player open-world action adventure across current-gen consoles, PC, Mac, and cloud play.

cards.title: Release Status
cards.detail: Players can buy and start the campaign immediately.

cards.title: Launch Date
cards.detail: The retail release followed the September 2025 reveal window.

cards.title: Offline Play
cards.detail: Offline play works after initial setup and the day one patch.

cards.title: Cross-Save
cards.detail: Progress does not move between platforms.

items.title: PlayStation 5
items.detail: Current-gen console release.

items.title: PlayStation 5 Pro
items.detail: Enhanced console option.

items.title: Xbox Series X|S
items.detail: Console launch platform.

items.title: Xbox on PC
items.detail: PC ecosystem support.

items.title: Steam
items.detail: PC and Mac availability.

items.title: Epic Games Store
items.detail: Alternative PC storefront.

items.title: Mac App Store
items.detail: Dedicated Mac purchase path.

items.title: GeForce NOW
items.detail: Cloud streaming access.

modules.note: PC controller support includes Xbox controllers and DualSense over USB.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Trailer Hub
modules.summary: Start with the official launch trailer, then work backward through the release date reveal, feature videos, and long-form gameplay showcases to understand the game fast.

items.title: Official Launch Trailer
items.detail: The current best snapshot of the release build and campaign tone.

items.title: Release Date Reveal Trailer
items.detail: The September 24, 2025 State of Play announcement that locked the release window.

items.title: Feature Video #1: Kliff and the Open World of Pywel
items.detail: Best primer for the setting, travel scale, and narrative direction.

items.title: Feature Video #2: Combat and Progression

items.title: Feature Video #3: Life in Pywel

modules.title: Crimson Desert Beginner Guide
modules.summary: The first hours revolve around Hernand, early Greymane quests, weapon-driven combat, and the town-to-camp loop of gathering, crafting, cooking, and unlocking stronger tools.

items.title: Reach Hernand and stabilize the early loop
items.detail: Help townspeople, clear outlaw-held spots, and open up more activities and quest lines.

items.title: Learn the basic attack rhythm first
items.detail: Chain fast and heavy attacks, then layer in blocking, parrying, grabs, and throws.

items.title: Visit the early vendors that matter
items.detail: The smithy, equipment shop, tailor, tannery, grocer, butchery, and fishing shop all help define your early momentum.

items.title: Prioritize gathering tools and cooking
items.detail: A fishing rod, pickaxe, and logging axe open the resource loop that feeds crafting and camp growth.

modules.note: Abyss Artifacts are the key early upgrade currency for unlocking stronger abilities and combat options.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Story Explained
modules.summary: Crimson Desert follows Kliff and the Greymanes across Pywel after the Black Bears shatter the fragile balance of Pailune, scattering the survivors across the continent.

items.title: Pywel is the campaign stage
items.detail: Cities, wilderness, ruins, and the Abyss all shape the route and stakes of the story.

items.title: Kliff leads the Greymanes
items.detail: Oongka, Yann, and Naira remain central names in the scattered survivor story.

items.title: The Black Bears trigger the collapse
items.detail: Their ambush breaks the balance in Pailune and forces the surviving Greymanes into recovery and retaliation.

items.title: The Abyss expands the scope
items.detail: The story grows beyond simple revenge as hidden factions and supernatural forces come into view.

modules.note: Additional playable perspectives later in the journey include Oongka and Damiane.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Characters
modules.summary: Meet the core faces of Crimson Desert, from Kliff and his Greymane comrades to the enemies and factions driving the war across Pywel.

cards.title: Confirmed protagonist
cards.detail: Kliff is the central protagonist and a surviving Greymane determined to reunite his scattered comrades.

cards.title: Confirmed Greymane comrades
cards.detail: The official story setup confirms Oongka, Yann, and Naira as Kliff's core companions before the Black Bears' assault.

cards.title: Confirmed additional named character
cards.detail: Official cast materials confirm Damiane as a named character with dedicated voice performance.

cards.title: Main enemy faction
cards.detail: The Black Bears are the faction responsible for shattering the Greymanes and escalating the war.

cards.title: Named enemy leader
cards.detail: Myurdin is identified as the leader driving the Black Bears' campaign against the Greymanes.

cards.title: Core region in the opening conflict
cards.detail: The early conflict begins in Pailune, where rival factions once held a fragile balance.

items.title: Kliff
items.detail: A Greymane survivor who sets out to reunite his comrades, rebuild the fallen group, and reclaim what was lost.

items.title: Oongka
items.detail: One of Kliff's confirmed companions on the official story page and one of the characters highlighted in official cast materials.

items.title: Yann
items.detail: Listed on the official main site as one of the Greymanes who stood beside Kliff before the Black Bears struck.

items.title: Naira
items.detail: Listed on the official main site as one of Kliff's close Greymane allies in the opening story setup.

items.title: Damiane
items.detail: Featured in official cast materials and later referenced in patch notes tied to greatsword skill updates.

items.title: Myurdin
items.detail: The leader of the Black Bears and the figure behind the attack that left the Greymanes dead or scattered.

modules.note: This module keeps cast and faction identity explicit so users can jump from names to deeper story and relationship pages.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Open World and Regions
modules.summary: Explore Pywel through its cities, wilderness, ruins, floating mysteries, and settlement hubs that shape the game's main journey and side content.

cards.title: World name
cards.detail: Pywel is the seamless world where cities, frontiers, ruins, and high-conflict zones connect into one travel loop.

cards.title: Official world type
cards.detail: Official materials describe the world as broad, contiguous, and built for cross-region exploration.

cards.title: Early city area
cards.detail: Hernand is identified as an early bustling city area filled with quests, encounters, and side activities.

cards.title: Home region tied to the Greymanes
cards.detail: Pailune is tied to the Greymanes and the opening conflict that drives the campaign.

cards.title: Camp location
cards.detail: Howling Hill is a named camp location tied to dispatches, upgrades, and resource routines.

cards.title: Major realm beyond the surface
cards.detail: The Abyss is presented as a floating mystery realm linked to artifacts, traversal, and puzzle elements.

items.title: Hernand
items.detail: A busy starting-area city where quests, outlaw strongholds, and side activities quickly open up.

items.title: Howling Hill
items.detail: Location of the Greymane camp, a core base tied to dispatches, upgrades, and resource loops.

items.title: Pailune
items.detail: A core region in the story conflict and the Greymanes' home region later tied to reconstruction.

items.title: The Abyss
items.detail: A realm of floating islands, strange technology, magical artifacts, puzzles, and traversal-focused powers.

items.title: Demeniss and Delesyia
items.detail: Named destinations referenced in published game guides and item-location routes.

items.title: Tomasso, Varnia, and Urdavah
items.detail: Additional confirmed locations tied to books, gear references, and broader exploration paths.

modules.note: This module gives a clear regional spine for Pywel so readers can move from world-level context into location-specific guides.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Combat Guide
modules.summary: Crimson Desert uses fast, pressure-heavy melee combat built around timing, weapon choice, chained skills, and reading powerful enemies in close-range fights.

cards.title: Combat style
cards.detail: Official combat coverage describes a quick and visceral pace that rewards decisive execution.

cards.title: Core defensive tools
cards.detail: Defensive timing is critical when enemies pressure from multiple angles.

cards.title: Core offensive tools
cards.value: Fast strikes, heavy slashes, grabs, throws
cards.detail: The offensive toolkit supports both chained pressure and hard punish windows.

cards.title: Enemy pressure
cards.value: Foes can surround and overwhelm Kliff
cards.detail: Positioning and tempo control matter because encounters escalate quickly.

cards.title: Build system
cards.detail: Combat identity is driven by weapons and move choices rather than fixed class roles.

cards.title: Boss combat focus
cards.value: Read abilities, parry well, punish openings
cards.detail: Boss encounters reward pattern reading and precise counterplay under pressure.

items.title: Pressure Fighting
items.detail: Combat rewards constant movement and quick decisions because enemies actively crowd, flank, and pressure the player.

items.title: Parry Timing
items.detail: A well-timed block can knock enemies off-balance and create windows for follow-up attacks.

items.title: Freeform Offense
items.detail: Combat is not locked into one rhythm; weapon choice, skill chains, grabs, and directional freedom shape each encounter.

items.title: Boss Readiness
items.detail: Stronger enemies and bosses demand pattern reading, heavy punish windows, and confidence against artifact-powered attacks.

modules.note: This module gives a combat-first reference layer that helps users bridge from core mechanics into deeper encounter-specific strategy.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Weapons and Skills
modules.summary: Weapons and skills define how Crimson Desert plays: no fixed classes, a broad melee toolkit, ranged options, skill-tree growth, and learn-by-observing progression.

cards.title: Default early weapon setup
cards.detail: The baseline setup supports balanced offense, defense, and parry-focused combat flow.

cards.title: Other confirmed melee weapons
cards.detail: Melee options shift range, timing windows, and punish profiles across encounters.

cards.title: Confirmed ranged weapons
cards.detail: Ranged tools add flexibility for hunting, spacing, and encounter control.

cards.title: Skill growth methods
cards.value: Skill tree unlocks and Watch and Learn
cards.detail: Growth combines progression unlocks with combat observation to expand Kliff's move set.

cards.title: Named utility power
cards.detail: A Spirit-branch ability tied to Axiom Force that supports exploration and gathering utility.

cards.title: Force Current requirement
cards.value: Axiom Force Level 2, Force Palm Level 2, and 1 Abyss Artifact
cards.detail: This requirement snapshot gives players a practical upgrade target for unlocking the skill.

items.title: Sword and Shield
items.detail: Kliff begins with a sword-and-shield setup that supports balanced offense, defense, and parry-focused play.

items.title: Heavy Weapons
items.detail: Great swords, spears, and axes shift combat approach toward different ranges, timings, and punish patterns.

items.title: Ranged Utility
items.detail: Kliff's bow and Damiane's guns are confirmed ranged options, with hunting specifically called out in current guides.

items.title: Watch and Learn
items.detail: Kliff can gain moves by observing other fighters, including in combat, adding new actions directly to his repertoire.

items.title: Force Current
items.detail: A Spirit-branch ability that can break trees and mineral deposits after charging a heavy Axiom Force attack.

modules.note: This module is organized around weapon identity and skill growth so users can quickly map their combat style and upgrade priorities.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Boss Guide
modules.summary: Crimson Desert has officially revealed four major boss encounters, each built around a distinct visual theme and combat identity.

cards.title: Officially revealed bosses
cards.detail: The official lineup currently lists four named major boss encounters.

cards.title: Public boss battle footage
cards.detail: Official boss battle trailers were published during the gamescom 2024 showcase cycle.

cards.title: Boss lineup
cards.value: White Horn, Staglord, Reed Devil, Queen Stoneback Crab
cards.detail: Each confirmed boss highlights a different region tone and encounter style in Pywel.

panels.title: White Horn
panels.subtitle: Mountain spirit
details: The first boss shown in the official boss battle trailer series.
Presented as a towering spirit encounter with a heavy fantasy-monster feel.

panels.title: Staglord
panels.subtitle: Fallen king
details: Described by Pearl Abyss as a fallen king seated upon a forsaken throne.
Fits the game's darker royal ruin aesthetic and set-piece style boss design.

panels.title: Reed Devil
panels.subtitle: Hernand outcast
details: Introduced as a slum-dweller of Hernand trying to rewrite his fate.
Ties the boss roster directly to one of the key early cities in Pywel.

panels.title: Queen Stoneback Crab
panels.subtitle: Gem-covered creature
details: Presented as a monster adorned with some of the rarest gems in Pywel.
Adds a large creature-style encounter to the revealed boss lineup.

modules.note: The current official boss set includes White Horn, Staglord, Reed Devil, and Queen Stoneback Crab.
modules.ctaLabel: Watch the official boss battle trailers

modules.title: Crimson Desert Multiplayer and Offline
modules.summary: Crimson Desert is built as a single-player action-adventure. After the initial setup and day-one patch, the game can be played offline.

cards.title: Game type
cards.detail: Official storefront and FAQ pages position the game as a solo campaign experience.

cards.title: Offline play
cards.value: Available after initial setup and day-one patch
cards.detail: An internet connection is needed first, but gameplay can continue offline afterward.

cards.title: Cross-save
cards.detail: Progress does not transfer between supported platform ecosystems.

cards.title: PC controller support
cards.value: Xbox controllers and DualSense via USB
cards.detail: PC players can use Xbox controllers or a wired DualSense setup.

panels.title: Can you play offline?
panels.subtitle: Yes, after setup
details: An internet connection is required for the initial setup and day-one patch.
Once installation is complete, the game can be enjoyed offline.

panels.title: Is there multiplayer or co-op?
panels.subtitle: No multiplayer listed
details: The Epic Games Store product page lists Crimson Desert as Single Player.
Official FAQ materials focus on solo play across supported platforms.

panels.title: Does it support cross-save?
panels.subtitle: No
details: Cross-save is not available between platforms.

panels.title: Where can you play?
panels.subtitle: Current platform list
details: PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 5 Pro
Xbox Series X|S and Xbox on PC
Steam on PC and Mac, Epic Games Store, Mac App Store, and GeForce NOW

modules.note: This module answers the most searched launch question directly: single-player first, offline after setup, and no cross-save.
modules.ctaLabel: See the launch FAQ and platform details

modules.title: Crimson Desert System Requirements and Performance
modules.summary: On PC, Crimson Desert requires 150 GB of SSD storage, 16 GB RAM, and DirectX 12. The official FAQ also confirms support for DLSS, FSR, PSSR, and MetalFX depending on platform.

cards.title: PC storage
cards.detail: Storage planning is straightforward: an SSD with at least 150 GB free is required.

cards.title: PC memory
cards.detail: Both minimum and recommended official targets list 16 GB system memory.

cards.title: Graphics API
cards.detail: Windows builds are specified against DirectX 12 support.

cards.title: Intel Arc support
cards.detail: Current official FAQ notes do not list Intel Arc GPU support.

panels.title: PC Minimum
panels.subtitle: Entry requirement
details: OS: Windows 10 64-bit
CPU: Ryzen 5 2600X / Intel i5-8500
Memory: 16 GB RAM
GPU: RX 5500 XT / GTX 1060
DirectX: Version 12
Storage: 150 GB available space, SSD required

panels.title: PC Recommended
panels.subtitle: Smoother baseline
details: OS: Windows 10 64-bit
CPU: Ryzen 5 5600 / Intel i5-11600K
Memory: 16 GB RAM
GPU: RX 6700 XT / RTX 2080
DirectX: Version 12
Storage: 150 GB available space, SSD required

panels.title: Performance Tech by Platform
panels.subtitle: Upscaling and frame generation
details: PC: FSR 3/4, DLSS 4/4.5, AMD FSR Ray Regeneration, NVIDIA DLSS Ray Reconstruction
PS5 Pro: Upgraded PSSR
PS5 base model and Xbox Series X|S: FSR 3
Mac: MetalFX Upscaler, Frame Generation, and Denoiser

panels.title: Extra Notes
panels.subtitle: Useful setup info
details: Frame generation is supported on PC and Mac.
Recommended drivers: AMD 25.9.2+ and NVIDIA 581.29+.
Mac recommendation: macOS 26 Tahoe or later.

modules.note: This module consolidates PC baseline requirements and platform performance technologies into one launch troubleshooting checkpoint.
modules.ctaLabel: Check full PC, console, and Mac performance specs

modules.title: Crimson Desert Editions and Bonuses
modules.summary: Crimson Desert is sold in Standard, Deluxe, and Collector's packages, with platform bonuses and add-on packs split across editions.

cards.title: Core editions
cards.value: Standard Edition, Deluxe Edition, Collector's Edition
cards.detail: The launch catalog is split into three main package tiers.

cards.title: Pre-order bonus
cards.detail: Players pre-ordering the game can claim the Khaled Shield bonus item.

cards.title: PlayStation exclusive bonus
cards.detail: PlayStation purchase paths include a platform-exclusive armor set bonus.

cards.title: Separate post-launch add-on
cards.detail: Players owning the base game can buy the Deluxe Pack separately after launch.

panels.title: Standard Edition
panels.subtitle: Physical and digital
details: Includes the full game.
Pre-order bonus: Khaled Shield.
PlayStation editions add the Grotevant Plate Set.
The Mac version is available only in the Digital Standard Edition and does not include the pre-order bonus.

panels.title: Deluxe Edition
panels.subtitle: Physical and digital
details: Includes the full game plus the Deluxe Pack.
Deluxe Pack items: Balgran Shield, Kairos Plate Set, Exclaire Horse Tack Set.
Physical Deluxe Edition also includes a SteelBook, Map of Pywel, Letter from the Developer, 3 Character Photo Cards, Greymane Brooch Pin, and 3 Patches.

panels.title: Collector's Edition
panels.subtitle: Physical only
details: Includes the full game, Deluxe Pack, and Ultimate Pack content.
Ultimate Pack items: Tormented Soul Bow, Derictus Spear, Sielos Longsword, Shroud Lantern, Hyperion Horse Tack Set.
Physical collector goods include a 17 x 11 x 10 inch diorama, Limited SteelBook, Fabric Map of Pywel, Letter from the Developer, 3 Character Photo Cards, Greymane Brooch Pin, and 3 Patches.

panels.title: Extra Bonus Notes
panels.subtitle: Store and upgrade details
details: The Deluxe Pack can be purchased separately after launch by players who already own the full game.
The Ultimate Pack from the Collector's Edition is not sold separately.
Epic Games Store buyers can also receive the Kliff Outfit in Fortnite as a gift-with-purchase offer shown on the store page.

modules.note: This module gives players a direct package comparison before purchase, including bonus boundaries and add-on availability.
modules.ctaLabel: Compare packages and claim your bonus items

modules.title: Crimson Desert Crafting and Cooking
modules.summary: Learn recipes, gather materials, craft stronger gear, and prepare better meals by combining town vendors, field tools, and camp support systems.

cards.title: Recipe learning method
cards.detail: Recipes are mainly learned from books and scrolls, then added to the Knowledge menu after examination.

cards.title: Vendor refresh cycle
cards.detail: Town vendor inventory refreshes on a daily in-game rhythm, making restock routes reliable.

cards.title: Meal quality tiers
cards.detail: Recovery meals scale by quality tier and become a practical progression lever.

cards.title: Confirmed gathering tools
cards.detail: These tools form the baseline loop for ingredient and material gathering.

panels.title: Core Gear Vendors
panels.subtitle: Town supply layer
details: Smithy
Equipment Shop
Tailor Shop
Tannery
Contribution Shop

panels.title: Core Food Sources
panels.subtitle: Ingredient layer
details: Butchery
Grocer
Fishing Shop
Street Shops
Wandering Merchants

panels.title: Force Current Gathering Unlock
panels.subtitle: Axiom Force route
details: Axiom Force Level 2
Force Palm Level 2

Enables tree and mineral gathering through Force Current

panels.title: Early Recipe Samples

Braised Fish and Pan-Fried Rice Cakes

items.detail: Early recovery option from basic fish and staple ingredients.

items.title: Braised Fish
items.detail: A stronger cooked dish commonly used in the early-to-mid progression loop.

items.title: Fishball Soup

items.title: Braised Ribs

modules.title: Crimson Desert Greymane Camp Guide
modules.summary: Build up Howling Hill, recruit scattered Greymanes, run dispatch missions, and turn the camp into a long-term resource and inventory engine.

cards.title: Camp unlock chapter
cards.detail: The camp loop begins in Chapter 3 and becomes a core progression layer.

cards.title: First expansion requirement
cards.detail: This is the first major threshold for expanding Howling Hill operations.

cards.title: Dispatch skill tags
cards.detail: Tags include Escort, Farmer, Rancher, Fisherman, Smith, Logger, Miner, Craftsman, Jeweler, Engineer, Cook, Explorer, Painter, and Weaver.

cards.title: Inventory reward chain
cards.detail: Greymane Commissions can reward Medium Bags worth +3 slots each.

items.title: Open Howling Hill and secure the first tent
items.detail: Start by securing your first tent, placing the banner, cooking 2 Modest Clear Soup, and bringing Marius back to camp.

items.title: Work through the three faction tabs
items.detail: Camp progression is structured around Scattered Embers, Grounds of the Sunrise, and Greymane Commissions.

items.title: Establish the camp management loop
items.detail: Cycle through Supply Chest rewards, Camp Provisions donations, recruit recovery, and dispatch missions to keep growth steady.

items.title: Scale dispatches by role and mission type
items.detail: Confirmed mission categories include Escort, Harvesting and Farming, Mining, and Logging.

items.title: Push expansion and inventory upgrades
items.detail: Complete commissions to stack Medium Bag rewards and reach larger settlement growth targets.

modules.note: This module frames camp management as a repeatable progression engine that feeds materials, roster strength, and inventory growth.
modules.ctaLabel: See the full Greymane Camp and Recruits guide

modules.title: Crimson Desert Side Activities and Exploration
modules.summary: Explore Pywel beyond the main story with liberated outposts, fishing loops, traversal upgrades, Abyss puzzle spaces, and organic world encounters.

cards.title: Liberation impact
cards.detail: Clearing outlaw-held spots restores civilian access and unlocks fresh quests and activity routes.

cards.title: Traversal kit
cards.detail: Movement systems share stamina management and shape how fast players can map new routes.

cards.title: Long-range mobility
cards.detail: Mount mobility supports rapid cross-region travel while mount and glider stamina improve over time.

cards.title: Upgrade catalyst
cards.detail: Exploration rewards feed progression by unlocking stronger abilities and systems.

items.title: Outpost Liberation
items.detail: Reclaim outlaw-controlled areas to reopen locations for townspeople and trigger additional activity chains.

items.title: Fish Market Recovery Loop
items.detail: A reclaimed fish market in Hernand brings fishermen back and opens the path for learning fishing by observation.

items.title: Abyss Puzzle Systems
items.detail: The Abyss introduces device-based puzzles, weight mechanics, heavy-object movement, and glider-driven traversal paths.

items.title: Short-Form Encounter Hunting
items.detail: Exploration naturally surfaces cliff rescues, hidden chests, and trade-related discoveries during normal travel.

items.title: Seamless World Discovery
items.detail: Official positioning emphasizes a vast seamless world spanning cities, wilderness, ruins, and Abyss routes.

modules.note: This module shows why exploration is not filler: each activity loop contributes to quests, resources, and long-term progression options.
modules.ctaLabel: Browse exploration, activities, and region guides

faq.title: Crimson Desert Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for the launch questions players keep asking.

items.question: What kind of game is Crimson Desert?
items.answer: Crimson Desert is a single-player open-world action adventure centered on Kliff, the Greymanes, and the continent of Pywel.

items.question: Is Crimson Desert multiplayer or co-op?
items.answer: Crimson Desert is not a multiplayer or co-op game. It is built as a single-player campaign.

items.question: Can Crimson Desert be played offline?
items.answer: Crimson Desert supports offline play after initial setup and the day one patch are completed.

items.question: Does Crimson Desert support cross-save?
items.answer: Crimson Desert does not support cross-save between platforms.

items.question: Who is the main character in Crimson Desert?
items.answer: Kliff is the central lead in Crimson Desert, with the Greymanes forming the core faction around him.

items.question: What should I learn first in Crimson Desert combat?
items.answer: In Crimson Desert, start by learning the rhythm between fast attacks, heavy attacks, blocking, parrying, and movement under pressure.

items.question: Where should I track official Crimson Desert updates?
items.answer: Use the official notices page, the official YouTube channel, the official X account, and the official Discord for ongoing Crimson Desert updates.
